question: The biquadratic (quadrupolar) planar spin model is defined on a stacked triangular lattice (STL) with Hamiltonian H' = -J' * sum_{<i,j>} cos^2(phi_i - phi_j), where phi_i is the spin angle, with antiferroquadrupolar coupling J'<0 and positive interplane coupling. Monte Carlo finite-size scaling is used to measure the critical indices nu_q and gamma_q. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature phase transition of the antiferroquadrupolar (J'<0) biquadratic exchange model on the stacked triangular lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". Also include "universality_class" using one of: "3D XY", "chiral", "2D Ising", "3D Heisenberg", "other".

metadata.system: Antiferroquadrupolar biquadratic planar spin model on stacked triangular lattice, J'<0
metadata.source_excerpt: The antiferroquadrupolar biquadratic exchange model on the stacked triangular lattice (STL)... undergoes a second-order transition. Finite-size scaling analysis yields critical indices nu_q=0.520±0.003 and gamma_q=1.072±0.009... consistent with chiral universality values
metadata.difficulty_note: Nonchiral model (no ordered plaquette chirality) exhibits chiral universality class behavior; frustration/noncollinearity rather than plaquette chirality determines the universality class.
